title: Why did the whole story happen
display_title: Why did the whole story happen
wikitext:
[]: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Tue Apr 20, 2010 8:35 am Reichu's translation of the NGE2 game is beginning to yield some rather interesting insights. The FAR is facing extinction, so they use their technology to create the Seeds of Life and the White/Black moons, and then evacuate the souls of all their homeworld's life into them. The Moons are sent off into space to preserve their legacy at the cost of their former culture being forgotten. The Dead Sea Scrolls are written as a record of their former civilization and as a technical manual for their technology. From this it can be inferred that they invented their own Angels/Evangelions for whatever purpose just like the Mu invented the RahXephon/Dolems in RahXephon. The Angels/Evangelions were also preserved in their own Seeds. Highly reminiscent of the new Battlestar Galactica, this. After the Moons go off into space, Lilith hijacks her seed in order to catch up to Adam and steal his Fruit of Life, which she lacks. Her reasons for doing this are unknown, and such a thing is considered blasphemous by the other FAR (hence why they didn't do it themselves?). First impact causes severe damage to Adam and he suffers amnesia. Lilith remembers, but is trapped within the Black Moon and forced to wait for billions of years to get her chance at the Fruit of Life. Then billions of years later the series happens and Adam and Lilith's children enter into a pointless war before the two Seeds merge into one creature, combining their respective souls into an LCL sea and bringing an end to the conflict. The future of the souls of the Lilin and the Angels is left intentionally vague.

[]:             Quote Post by Cypher » Tue Apr 20, 2010 9:42 am Wasn't it that, Liliths Black Moon crashed into Earth because two seeds were accidently sent to the same planet. Also in the crash Lilith's lance was damaged and she was forced to use Adam's to impale him and put him into a suspended animation while she replaced Angel based life with her Lilim.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Apr 20, 2010 10:03 am I'm not aware of anything canon that says Lilith followed Adam to Earth deliberately. Is that in Reichu's NGE2 translations? Lilith did not use the Lance on Adam; the "Duel of the Seeds" was disproved ages ago. Adam's Lance acted on its own in suspending Adam. It was enforcing the "one seed per world" rule.

[]: Re: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Apr 20, 2010 10:26 am I'm not aware of anything canon that says Lilith followed Adam to Earth deliberately. Is that in Reichu's NGE2 translations?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Apr 20, 2010 10:41 am I don't think Zenoseiya's talking about the C.I., but rather about Reichu's more recent translations of the game scenarios themselves. [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8703[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 20, 2010 2:21 pm The Dead Sea Scrolls are written as a record of their former civilization and as a technical manual for their technology. From this it can be inferred that they invented their own Angels/Evangelions for whatever purpose just like the Mu invented the RahXephon/Dolems in RahXephon. The Angels/Evangelions were also preserved in their own Seeds. Highly reminiscent of the new Battlestar Galactica, this.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Apr 20, 2010 2:27 pm This is quite interesting, however I was never concerned with the why, just with the events caused and the characters we know.

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Tue Apr 20, 2010 4:22 pm Part of these statements are based on the scenarios the NGE2 game, which is where we got the whole FAR-shtick from in the first place. I think the information is equally valid whether it came from the supplemental material in the game or the actual game storyline. AuraTwilight wrote: Wait, hold on, you're jumping ahead of yourself. Kaworu makes it pretty fucking obvious that Evangelions are a Lilin invention; just because the Dead Sea Scrolls assisted their production doesn't mean the FAR made their own; it's a simple recipe of "Adam's DNA equivalent + Technology notes on how to clone deities = Evangelions."

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 20, 2010 4:43 pm Part of these statements are based on the scenarios the NGE2 game, which is where we got the whole FAR-shtick from in the first place. I think the information is equally valid whether it came from the supplemental material in the game or the actual game storyline.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 20, 2010 7:34 pm This thread seems a bit premature... Translation is still underway. (Well, that's what I'm going to tell you, at least.) I don't think it's worth arguing about anything in e.g. Rei's PSP scenario (the one where Lilith is all "GIVE ME THE FRUIT OF LIFE!!!") until more context is provided. @Aura: I don't see how the bit about seven seeds contradicts anything we're told (on same canonical level or higher). Are you sure you're not mistaking actual contradiction with a personal preconception that there ought to be a whole lot more Seeds? Also, in Kaworu's PSP ending, the anonymous Seed seems to recall the past at least well enough to describe it in the usual obtuse NGE dialogue style.

[]: Re: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 20, 2010 11:02 pm Zenoseiya wrote: Lilith hijacks her seed in order to catch up to Adam and steal his Fruit of Life, which she lacks. First impact causes severe damage to Adam and he suffers amnesia. Lilith remembers, but is trapped within the Black Moon and forced to wait for billions of years to get her chance at the Fruit of Life.

[]: Re: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 21, 2010 12:05 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I don't know if this is true or not, but I swear, the more I read about the mythology behind everything the more it all seems like some intergalactic soap opera.

[]: Re: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Apr 21, 2010 2:02 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I swear, the more I read about the mythology behind everything the more it all seems like some intergalactic soap opera.

[]: Re: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Apr 21, 2010 2:13 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: But don't Roman or Greek mythologies do basically the same thing? I mean, just how many chicks- I mean Goddesses, did Zeus have relations with anyway?

[]: Re: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Apr 21, 2010 2:16 pm NemZ wrote: Greek mythology was the /b/ of the ancient world.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:02 pm Reichu wrote: @Aura: I don't see how the bit about seven seeds contradicts anything we're told (on same canonical level or higher). Are you sure you're not mistaking actual contradiction with a personal preconception that there ought to be a whole lot more Seeds?

[]: Re: Why did the whole story happen? [Spoilers]             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:03 pm Azathoth wrote: I kind of like this whole business with seven Seeds of Life, FAR shenanigans, etc.; it's all very space opera classical, and it brings me to one of the things I like most about Eva: it distills such remarkable characters and situations from a backstory that contains nothing but silliness.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Apr 22, 2010 1:14 pm What you detect is trolling.
